text.title: PMBoK 8 Changes and Agent Edition Drift
text.summary: Key changes from PMBoK 7 to PMBoK 8 and the agent failures that occur when training-data edition bias overrides the instructed edition.

list.item: Principles: 12 (PMBoK 7) streamlined to 6 (PMBoK 8): Stewardship and Ethics, Collaboration, Value Optimisation, Systems and Complexity, Adaptability, Sustainability
Performance Domains: 8 (PMBoK 7) reorganised to 7 (PMBoK 8): see seven-performance-domains methodology
Sustainability: moved from "mentioned" in PMBoK 7 to a core principle in PMBoK 8; requires a measurable proxy (CO2 per release, accessibility audit pass rate)
AI in PM: no coverage in PMBoK 7; dedicated appendix in PMBoK 8 covering schedule prediction, risk analysis, resource optimisation, status reporting
Processes: none (fully principle-based in PMBoK 7) to 40 non-prescriptive processes in PMBoK 8
Quality: integrated throughout all domains in both editions; never a standalone domain
Communications: merged into Stakeholders domain in PMBoK 8
Procurement: moved to appendix in PMBoK 8; no longer a standalone domain

rule.statement: Pin the PMBoK edition in every project charter and in every agent system prompt that generates PM content. Never let an agent assume the edition.
rule.rationale: PMBoK 6 is over-represented in training data. Without explicit pinning, agents default to the 6th edition vocabulary (knowledge areas, process groups) regardless of instruction.

antipattern.description: Agent output contains "Integration Management", "Cost Management", or "Communications Management" as domain names.
antipattern.reason: These are PMBoK 6 knowledge area names. In PMBoK 7/8 these are replaced by Governance, Finance, and integrated Stakeholders respectively. Output containing these names must be regenerated with PMBoK 7/8 vocabulary pinned.

antipattern.description: Agent applies EVM metrics to a pure-agile project with no scope or cost baseline.
antipattern.reason: EVM requires a baseline. Without one, SPI and CPI are undefined. Agile metrics (cycle time, flow efficiency, throughput) must be used instead.

antipattern.description: "RAG" used without spelling out Red/Amber/Green on first use in PM contexts.
antipattern.reason: "RAG" is also retrieval-augmented generation. In PM system prompts, abbreviation without disambiguation causes agents to hallucinate RAG retrieval references in status reports.
